name: Anthropic API
notes: Pay-as-you-go; not a traditional subscription. Users searching for how to stop charges.

name: Carvana
cancel_steps: Carvana is primarily a used car marketplace — not a recurring subscription. Most users are looking to return a recently purchased vehicle.
Vehicle return: Carvana offers a 7-day return policy on vehicles purchased. Go to carvana.com > My Account > Manage Your Vehicle > Return Vehicle.
For Carvana Care (vehicle protection plan): call 1-800-333-4554 or manage at carvana.com/account. Carvana Care is the recurring subscription component — vehicle protection / extended warranty.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None significant.
notes: Carvana is not a traditional subscription service. If you're here for: (1) vehicle return — use the 7-day return policy; (2) Carvana Care cancellation — call or manage online; (3) financing — contact your lender. The 7-day return window is a real consumer protection; use it if you have buyer's remorse.

name: Connected Car Subscriptions (GM / OnStar)
cancel_steps: Press the blue OnStar button in your GM vehicle and request cancellation, OR call 1-888-4ONSTAR (1-888-466-7827). See also the dedicated OnStar entry at cancelfreely.com/cancel/onstar for the full OnStar-specific guide.
For other GM connected services (WiFi hotspot, Remote Access, Super Cruise): go to account.gm.com > Subscriptions > manage individual services.
State which service(s) you want to cancel. Agents will make retention offers.
known_friction: GM vehicles may have several separate connected service subscriptions: OnStar safety/security, WiFi hotspot (AT&T), Remote Access (app features), and Super Cruise (driver assist). Each may bill separately and cancel separately. Users often don't know how many they have. | Retention tactics: Offers discounted rates for individual services.; Offers to keep one service while canceling others.
notes: GM connected services cover OnStar, WiFi hotspot, Remote Access, and Super Cruise across Chevrolet, GMC, Buick, and Cadillac brands. Some services are free trials that auto-convert — check account.gm.com to see all active subscriptions. The OnStar entry in our database covers the primary safety/security subscription in more detail.

name: Endurance Warranty
cancel_steps: Call Endurance: 1-866-432-4443.

To cancel, you must mail a written cancellation request along with a Federal Odometer Statement or notarized mileage statement to the administrator listed in your contract. The mileage documentation requirement is unusual and adds significant friction compared to standard subscription cancellations.
Within 30 days of purchase: full refund. After 30 days: prorated refund minus a cancellation fee (typically $75-$150). 30-day free look period is standard in the vehicle warranty industry.
known_friction: Endurance requires a Federal Odometer Statement or notarized mileage statement to process cancellations — a bureaucratic hurdle not found in standard subscription services. This requirement slows the cancellation process significantly.; Endurance has a 30-day/1,000-mile waiting period before coverage activates. Customers who cancel before coverage begins may not realize they were never actually covered. | Retention tactics: May offer a modified plan or payment restructuring.

free_alternatives.name: Your manufacturer warranty
free_alternatives.note: Check remaining factory warranty before purchasing extended coverage

notes: Endurance is a third-party vehicle service contract provider. The prorated refund is standard. Avoid all third-party warranty companies that contact you via robocall or unsolicited mail — these are frequently scams. Endurance is a legitimate company but cancellation requires more documentation than typical subscriptions.

name: Toyota Connected Services
cancel_steps: Go to toyota.com/connected-services and sign in to your Toyota account.

Navigate to Manage Services / Subscriptions and select the service to cancel. Toyota connected services include Remote Connect, Safety Connect, Wi-Fi Connect, Drive Connect (navigation), and Destination Assist.
Alternatively, call Toyota Connected Services support: 1-800-331-4331. Phone may be required for some service types.
known_friction: New Toyota vehicles include free trials of connected services (typically 1-3 years). When trials expire, services auto-convert to paid subscriptions. Many owners are surprised by the charge and don't realize they signed up.; Toyota's connected services are sold as separate subscriptions (Remote Connect, Safety Connect, etc.), each billing and canceling independently. Owners may not know how many active subscriptions they have. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal rate.
notes: Toyota connected services vary by model year and trim. Check toyota.com/connected-services to see which services are available for your specific vehicle. Free trial periods vary — Remote Connect may be 3 years free on newer models. After the trial, each service bills separately.

name: Backblaze
cancel_steps: Go to backblaze.com and sign in.

Go to 'My Account' > 'Overview' > find your backup license and click 'Remove'. For Computer Backup: remove all registered backups and licenses. For B2 Cloud Storage: the account can be canceled separately.
To fully delete your account: go to 'My Account' > 'Account Details' > 'Close Account'.
known_friction: Backblaze explicitly states no refunds on monthly subscriptions. Cancel at the end of your billing period to avoid paying for unused time. | Retention tactics: None significant.
notes: Backblaze is straightforward to cancel. No refunds on monthly plans — time your cancellation to the end of your billing period. Annual/2-year plans get a 30-day refund window. Before canceling, download your backed-up data if needed — you can request a hard drive restore for a fee.

name: Carbonite
cancel_steps: Go to carbonite.com and sign in to your account.
Go to 'Account Summary' and find the auto-renewal setting. Turn off automatic renewal. Your subscription remains active through the paid period, then expires.
⚠️ CRITICAL: After your subscription expires, Carbonite permanently deletes your backed-up files after a grace period of 30–60 days. Download any important backed-up files before canceling.
known_friction: Backed-up files are permanently deleted 30–60 days after subscription expiry. This is not prominently warned during the cancellation flow. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal rate.
notes: Carbonite is an online backup service (files, not apps). The data deletion window is the critical thing — don't cancel without first downloading your backed-up files or ensuring you have local copies. Carbonite is owned by OpenText as of 2019.

name: Google One
cancel_steps: Go to one.google.com and sign in.
Click 'Manage' next to your storage plan, then select 'Cancel plan'.
On Android: Open Google One app > Settings > Manage Google One > Cancel membership.

Access reverts to the free 15GB tier. Files above that limit become inaccessible to upload/edit — but existing files are not deleted for a grace period. Prorated refund is available if you request it immediately after canceling.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a downgrade to a lower storage tier.

free_alternatives.name: Google (free 15GB)
free_alternatives.note: Free 15GB shared across Gmail, Drive, and Photos

notes: Google One is the storage tier upgrade for Gmail/Drive/Photos. Canceling reverts to the free 15GB. If you're over 15GB, prioritize cleaning up before canceling to avoid access issues.

name: iCloud+
cancel_steps: On iPhone/iPad: Go to Settings > [your name] > iCloud > Manage Account Storage > Change Storage Plan > Downgrade Options > select Free.
On Mac: System Settings > [your name] > iCloud > Manage > Change Storage Plan > Downgrade Options.
Without an Apple device: Go to appleid.apple.com, sign in, then manage subscriptions.

⚠️ Downgrading takes effect at the end of the billing period. Files above the free 5GB limit won't be deleted immediately, but new uploads and iCloud backups will stop working until you're under the limit.
known_friction: Apple's cancellation flow doesn't prominently surface what happens to data above 5GB after downgrade. Users who don't clean up first may lose access to backups and find devices can't back up. | Retention tactics: None — Apple's flow is clean.
notes: iCloud+ plans start at $0.99/month for 50GB. Before downgrading, clean out iCloud Drive, delete old device backups, and optimize photo storage. Family Sharing members share the same storage plan — downgrading affects everyone.

name: OneDrive (Microsoft 365)
cancel_steps: Go to account.microsoft.com and sign in. OneDrive storage plans are bundled with Microsoft 365. Canceling Microsoft 365 also cancels the extra storage.
Go to 'Services & subscriptions' > find your Microsoft 365 plan > 'Manage' > 'Cancel subscription'.

After cancellation, OneDrive reverts to the free 5GB tier. Files above that limit become read-only and then inaccessible after a grace period. ⚠️ Microsoft is ending standalone SharePoint Online and OneDrive plans as of May 2026 — only Microsoft 365 bundles will be available going forward.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal.
notes: Standalone OneDrive storage plans are being discontinued by Microsoft (new sales end May 31, 2026). Storage above 5GB requires a Microsoft 365 subscription. After cancellation, download any files above the 5GB limit before they become inaccessible.

name: Bumble
cancel_steps: ⚠️ Deleting the Bumble app does NOT cancel your subscription.
iOS: Open Settings > [your name] > Subscriptions > Bumble > Cancel Subscription.
Android: Open Google Play > Profile > Payments & Subscriptions > Subscriptions > Bumble > Cancel.
Web: Go to bumble.com > Profile photo > Manage your Boost or Manage your Premium > Cancel. Web cancellation is available for direct/web subscriptions.
known_friction: Like most dating apps, deleting Bumble does not cancel the subscription. Must cancel through the originating platform. | Retention tactics: None significant beyond standard confirmation.

free_alternatives.name: Bumble (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free tier with limited swipes and features

notes: Bumble's cancellation is cleaner than most dating apps — web cancellation works for web subscribers. Covers Bumble Boost, Premium, and Premium+ tiers. Cancel method depends entirely on where you originally subscribed.

name: eHarmony
cancel_steps: Go to eharmony.com and sign in.
Click your profile icon > Account Settings > Billing.
Find 'Cancel Subscription' or 'Turn Off Automatic Renewal'. eHarmony frames cancellation as 'turning off auto-renewal' rather than canceling. Your subscription continues until the current term ends.
You will be required to enter your password and navigate multiple confirmation screens before cancellation is processed. Multiple screens with retention messaging before the final confirmation.
If subscribed via Apple or Google, cancel through those platforms.
known_friction: eHarmony presents cancellation as 'turning off automatic renewal' rather than 'canceling your subscription' — obscuring the action and creating uncertainty about whether the account has been truly canceled.; eHarmony's cancellation flow includes password re-entry and multiple confirmation screens with retention messaging before reaching final cancel.; eHarmony charges $35.90-$65.90/month and auto-renews at full price after introductory offers expire. Many users report surprise charges. | Retenti
notes: eHarmony is one of the more friction-heavy dating app cancels. The 'turn off auto-renewal' framing is intentionally ambiguous — confirm you've received a cancellation confirmation email. Prices are high ($35.90-$65.90/mo) making the auto-renewal trap particularly costly.

name: Hinge
cancel_steps: ⚠️ Deleting your Hinge account or the app does NOT cancel your subscription. You must cancel through the platform where you subscribed.
iOS: Open Settings > [your name] > Subscriptions > Hinge > Cancel Subscription.
Android: Open Google Play > Profile > Payments & Subscriptions > Subscriptions > Hinge > Cancel.
Access continues through end of billing period.
known_friction: Deleting the Hinge account or app does not cancel the paid subscription. Users must cancel through Apple or Google separately — a common trap. | Retention tactics: None — Apple/Google handle the flow.

free_alternatives.name: Hinge (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free tier with 8 daily likes — functional for most users

notes: Hinge (owned by Match Group) is generally considered one of the better-designed dating apps. The free tier is genuinely usable. The subscription (Hinge+ or HingeX) cancels through Apple/Google. No refund for unused time.

name: Match.com
cancel_steps: Go to match.com and sign in. If subscribed via Apple or Google, cancel through those platforms instead.
Click your profile photo > Settings > Subscription.
Find 'Cancel Subscription' or 'Manage Subscription' and follow the prompts. Match requires navigating through several menus — the cancel option is not prominently displayed.
⚠️ Canceling stops auto-renewal but your subscription remains active until the end of the paid term. Match does not issue refunds for unused time.
known_friction: Match.com does not refund unused subscription time after cancellation. Canceling stops future charges but does not recover the current period.; Match's cancel flow turns off auto-renewal but doesn't make clear that the subscription itself continues until the paid period ends. Users sometimes believe they've gotten a refund when they haven't. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal.
notes: Match is owned by Match Group (same company as Tinder, Hinge, OkCupid). No refund for unused time is the main gotcha — time cancellation carefully to near the end of your billing period.

name: Tinder
cancel_steps: ⚠️ CRITICAL: Deleting the Tinder app does NOT cancel your subscription. You will keep being charged.
iOS: Open Settings > [your name] > Subscriptions > Tinder > Cancel Subscription.
Android: Open Google Play > Profile > Payments & Subscriptions > Subscriptions > Tinder > Cancel.
Web (if subscribed via tinder.com): Go to tinder.com > Profile > Settings > Manage Payment Account > Cancel Subscription.
known_friction: Deleting the Tinder app does not cancel the subscription — charges continue. This trips up a large number of users who assume deletion = cancellation.; You must cancel through the same platform where you subscribed (Apple, Google, or web). If you subscribed via iOS and try to cancel in the app, there is no cancel button — you must go to Apple Settings. | Retention tactics: None at the app store level — Apple/Google handle the flow.

free_alternatives.name: Tinder (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with limited likes and features

notes: The app deletion trap is Tinder's most common gotcha. Always cancel through Apple/Google settings, not by deleting the app. Tinder offers Plus, Gold, and Platinum tiers — all cancel through the same platform-specific flow.

name: Chegg
cancel_steps: Go to chegg.com and sign in.

Go to Account > Subscriptions > select your plan > Cancel Subscription. Navigate through any retention screens — Chegg may show pause options, surveys, and discount offers before completing cancellation.
Get a cancellation confirmation email. Chegg has a documented history of continuing charges after cancellation — monitor your next statement.
known_friction: Chegg paid $7.5M to settle FTC charges in September 2025 for unlawful cancellation practices. The FTC found hidden links, pre-selected 'pause' options, mandatory surveys, and repeated attempts to dissuade cancellation — and continued charging customers who believed they had canceled.; Multiple users reported being charged after confirmed cancellation. Chegg's post-settlement flow has improved but get confirmation and check your next statement.; Chegg's pre-settlement cancellation flow required n

free_alternatives.name: Khan Academy (free)
free_alternatives.note: Free tutoring and practice for many subjects

notes: Chegg settled with the FTC for $7.5M in September 2025 over illegal dark patterns. Post-settlement, the cancellation flow is required to be cleaner — but get a confirmation email and verify no further charges. If charged after cancellation, dispute with your bank and file an FTC complaint at reportfraud.ftc.gov.

name: Coursera Plus
cancel_steps: Go to coursera.org and sign in.
Click your profile icon > Settings > Subscriptions > Cancel subscription.
If within 14 days of purchase: request a full refund — Coursera Plus has a 14-day money-back guarantee. After 14 days, no refund is available. Access continues through end of billing period.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a pause or discounted rate.

free_alternatives.name: Coursera (free audit)
free_alternatives.note: Many courses can be audited for free without a certificate

free_alternatives.name: edX (free audit)
free_alternatives.note: Similar course platform with free audit option

notes: Coursera Plus ($59/month or $399/year) gives unlimited access to 7,000+ courses. 14-day refund window is genuine. Course progress and certificates earned are retained after cancellation.

name: Duolingo Plus
cancel_steps: Cancel method depends on where you subscribed.
iOS: Settings > [your name] > Subscriptions > Duolingo > Cancel.
Android: Google Play > Profile > Payments & Subscriptions > Subscriptions > Duolingo > Cancel.
Web: duolingo.com > Profile > Settings > Super Duolingo > Cancel subscription.
Your streak and progress are preserved after canceling — you revert to the free tier with ads.
known_friction: Deleting the Duolingo app does not cancel the subscription. Must cancel through originating platform. | Retention tactics: None significant.

free_alternatives.name: Duolingo (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with ads; all core language learning content included

notes: Duolingo Plus/Super Duolingo ($6.99-$9.99/month) removes ads and adds streak protection. The free tier includes all language content — Plus is a convenience upgrade, not a content gate. Streak and progress survive cancellation.

name: LinkedIn Learning
cancel_steps: Go to linkedin.com and sign in. LinkedIn Learning is bundled with LinkedIn Premium — canceling Learning cancels the Premium subscription too.
Go to your profile > Premium hub > Cancel subscription. Or go to linkedin.com/premium/manage and select cancel.
Access continues through end of billing period. Certificates earned are retained on your profile.
known_friction: LinkedIn Learning is not a standalone product — it's included in LinkedIn Premium. You cannot cancel Learning without canceling the full Premium subscription. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal.

free_alternatives.name: Coursera (free audit)
free_alternatives.note: Free audit access to many courses

notes: LinkedIn Learning is included with LinkedIn Premium Career ($39.99/month) and Premium Business ($59.99/month). Canceling Premium cancels Learning access. Certificates you've already earned remain on your LinkedIn profile regardless of subscription status.

name: MasterClass
cancel_steps: Go to masterclass.com and sign in.
Go to Account > Settings > Cancel Subscription (or 'Cancel Membership Renewal').
MasterClass has a 30-day satisfaction guarantee — if within 30 days, contact support for a refund. After 30 days, no refund is available. Access continues through end of billing period.
If subscribed via a third-party marketplace or gift card, MasterClass will not process cancellations — contact that marketplace directly.
known_friction: MasterClass primarily sells annual plans. The renewal date arrives once a year, making it easy to forget to cancel before being charged for another full year.; MasterClass explicitly disclaims responsibility for subscriptions purchased through third-party marketplaces — customers must handle cancellation through wherever they purchased. | Retention tactics: May offer a pause or discounted renewal.

free_alternatives.name: YouTube (free)
free_alternatives.note: Many MasterClass instructors also have free YouTube content

notes: MasterClass ($120-$180/year) is annual-billed. Set a calendar reminder 30 days before renewal to decide whether to keep it. The 30-day guarantee is genuine. After the annual renewal date passes with no cancellation, you're committed to another full year.

name: Rosetta Stone
cancel_steps: Go to rosettastone.com and sign in.
Go to Account > Subscriptions > Cancel subscription. If subscribed via Apple or Google Play, cancel through those platforms.
Access continues through end of billing period.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a discounted rate or pause.
notes: Rosetta Stone ($11.99-$14.99/month or lifetime purchase) is one of the older language learning platforms. The lifetime purchase model means some users have a one-time purchase rather than a subscription — those don't need cancellation. Cancel the subscription only; the lifetime license is permanent.

name: Skillshare
cancel_steps: Go to skillshare.com and sign in. If subscribed via iOS or Android, cancel through Apple/Google instead.
Go to Account > Membership & Payments > Cancel Membership.
Access continues through end of billing period. No refund for unused time.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal or pause.
notes: Skillshare ($32/month or $168/year) is a creative skills platform (design, illustration, photography, etc.). Clean cancellation. No refund for unused time — time your cancellation to near the end of your billing period.

name: Udemy Pro
cancel_steps: Go to udemy.com and sign in.
Go to Account > Subscriptions > find your plan > Cancel subscription. If subscribed via Apple or Google Play, cancel through those platforms instead.
Access continues through end of billing period. Courses previously purchased individually are retained.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a discounted renewal.

free_alternatives.name: Udemy (individual course purchase)
free_alternatives.note: Individual courses frequently on sale for $10-15; no subscription needed

free_alternatives.name: YouTube (free tutorials)
free_alternatives.note: Free video tutorials on most topics

notes: Udemy's subscription (Personal Plan) is distinct from individual course purchases. Canceling the subscription does not remove courses you've individually purchased — those are yours permanently. The subscription gives unlimited access to the full catalog while active.

name: Fastmail
cancel_steps: Go to fastmail.com and sign in.
Go to Settings > Billing > Cancel account. If you have multiple users on your account, delete all other users first before you can cancel.
No further payments required after cancellation. Export your email data first (Settings > Export).
known_friction: Retention tactics: None — Fastmail sends renewal reminders and makes cancellation clean.
notes: Fastmail ($3-$9/month depending on plan) is a privacy-respecting email provider based in Australia. Clean cancellation with advance renewal reminders. Export your email before canceling — Settings > Export gives you a full MBOX backup.

name: Google Workspace Individual
cancel_steps: Go to workspace.google.com and sign in with your Google account. Note: Google Workspace Individual (personal) is different from Google Workspace Business/Enterprise (admin console). These instructions are for Individual.
Go to Settings > Subscriptions > Cancel subscription. Or go to support.google.com/google-workspace-individual and follow the cancellation link.
Access continues through end of billing period. Email, Meet, and other features revert to standard free Google account limits.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None significant.
notes: Google Workspace Individual ($9.99/month) adds Meet premium features, custom email domains, and enhanced storage to a personal Google account. For Business/Enterprise plans, cancellation is in the Admin console (different process). After canceling Individual, your Google account continues normally on the free tier.

name: Proton Mail Plus
cancel_steps: Go to proton.me and sign in.
Click your profile icon > All settings > Dashboard > Downgrade plan. You're downgrading to the free tier — Proton doesn't have a traditional 'cancel' since the free plan continues.
Select the free plan. If you exceed free storage limits after downgrade, you'll need to delete emails/files to get under the cap.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None.
notes: Proton Mail is on the /alternatives page as a trusted service. Cancellation is a downgrade to the free tier — clean, no friction. Free tier includes 1GB storage and 1 email address. Paid plans start at $3.99/month (Mail Plus) with more storage and addresses.

name: Skype Premium
cancel_steps: Skype shut down on May 5, 2025. There is nothing to cancel. Microsoft ended Skype after 14 years. All calling subscriptions were automatically terminated.
Any Skype Credit remaining was available for use until the shutdown date. Subscriptions extending beyond May 1, 2025 were automatically canceled and converted to Skype Credit where applicable.
If you're still seeing charges from Skype after May 2025, dispute them with your bank.
notes: Skype officially shut down May 5, 2025. Microsoft migrated users to Microsoft Teams. If you had a Skype Number, you needed to port it to another carrier before shutdown. Any ongoing charges after May 2025 are unauthorized — dispute with your bank.

name: Superhuman
cancel_steps: In the Superhuman app (Mac): press Cmd+K > type 'Cancel Subscription' > follow prompts.
Or go to superhuman.com/admin and cancel from the billing section.
If you can't find the cancel option, email support@superhuman.com. Team/contracted accounts have a different cancellation process — contact your account manager.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None significant.
notes: Superhuman ($30/month) is a premium email client focused on speed. Clean cancellation. After canceling, your underlying email account (Gmail, Outlook, etc.) is unaffected — you just lose the Superhuman interface.

name: Acorns
cancel_steps: Open the Acorns app or go to app.acorns.com and sign in.
Tap your profile icon > Settings > Personal > Subscription > Close Account.
Follow the prompts to withdraw your balance and confirm account closure. Your invested balance will be liquidated and returned to your linked bank account. This may take 3-5 business days.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None significant.

free_alternatives.name: Fidelity (free)
free_alternatives.note: Free investing with no account minimums

notes: Acorns ($3-$5/month) is a micro-investing app. Account closure is in-app and straightforward. Your invested balance is liquidated and returned to your bank — this may trigger capital gains taxes if your investments have appreciated. Canceling is immediate; withdrawal takes a few business days.

name: Credit Karma
cancel_steps: Credit Karma is free — there is no paid subscription to cancel. To delete your account: go to creditkarma.com and sign in.
Go to Settings > Account > 'Close Account'. The close account option is somewhat buried — Reddit users note it requires some searching.

Follow the prompts to confirm account deletion. Credit Karma will delete your account and personal data. Note: if you have a Credit Karma Money account (savings or spending), that must be closed separately first.
known_friction: The account deletion option is not prominently surfaced. Reddit users describe it as 'a bit tricky' to find. Credit Karma (owned by Intuit) monetizes through financial product referrals — account closure is not incentivized. | Retention tactics: None — no paid subscription.
notes: Credit Karma has no paid subscription — it's free and makes money through financial product referrals (credit cards, loans). If you want to stop using it, delete the account to remove your data. Close any Credit Karma Money accounts first before account deletion.

name: Mint (Intuit)
cancel_steps: Mint shut down on January 1, 2024. The service no longer exists. Intuit migrated Mint users to Credit Karma. There is nothing to cancel.
If you have a Credit Karma account created via Mint migration and want to close it: go to creditkarma.com > Settings > Account > Close Account.

free_alternatives.name: Credit Karma (free)
free_alternatives.note: Intuit's replacement — free budgeting and credit monitoring

free_alternatives.name: YNAB
free_alternatives.note: Paid, but the gold standard for zero-based budgeting

notes: Mint (Intuit) shut down permanently on January 1, 2024. Users were migrated to Credit Karma. If you're still seeing Mint charges, they are likely Credit Karma-related or an error — contact Intuit support.

name: Noom
cancel_steps: In the Noom app: tap your profile > Settings > Subscription > Cancel Subscription.
Online: go to noom.com > sign in > account settings > Subscription Portal > Cancel.

Navigate through multiple retention screens — Noom will offer free trial extensions, deep discounts, and pause options before allowing cancellation. Stay firm. Multiple Reddit users report needing to decline 3-5 offers before reaching the final cancel button.
Get a cancellation confirmation email — save it. Noom has been reported to continue charging after cancellation without confirmation.
known_friction: Multiple Reddit users report Noom continued renewing charges after what they believed was a successful cancellation, with no renewal notification emails. One user described discovering months of charges they never received receipts for.; Noom's cancellation flow includes multiple retention screens with escalating offers (free trials, deep discounts, pauses) that must each be declined before reaching the final cancel button.; Reddit users describe Noom as 'impossible to cancel,' with phone agents

free_alternatives.name: MyFitnessPal (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free calorie tracking and food logging

notes: Noom is one of the most complained-about health app cancellations. Always get a confirmation email and check your credit card statement for the following month. If you're charged after cancellation, file a chargeback and contact Noom support with your cancellation confirmation.

name: QuickBooks
cancel_steps: Sign in at quickbooks.intuit.com.
Click the gear icon (Settings) > Account and Settings > Billing & Subscription.
Click 'Cancel subscription' and follow the prompts. For QuickBooks Self-Employed: same path, or go directly to quickbooks.intuit.com/self-employed.
Export your data before canceling: Reports > export to Excel/CSV. Access ends at end of billing period. ⚠️ After cancellation, you lose access to your QuickBooks data. Export everything first.
known_friction: QuickBooks does not preserve data access after cancellation. Users who cancel without exporting lose access to years of financial records. This is not prominently warned in the cancellation flow. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted rate or pause.; Offers to downgrade to a lower plan.
notes: Export all data before canceling — this is critical. Run and download: P&L, Balance Sheet, Transaction List, and any custom reports you need. QuickBooks Desktop licenses work differently from QuickBooks Online subscriptions.

name: Robinhood
cancel_steps: Before closing: sell or transfer all stocks, ETFs, and crypto. Withdraw all cash to your bank account. IRA accounts require a rollover to another institution. You cannot close a Robinhood account with a balance. All positions must be liquidated or transferred first.
In the app: tap your profile icon > Account > Investing > Account Information > Deactivate Account.
On web: go to account.robinhood.com > Settings > Investing > Deactivate Account.

Send a written confirmation to support@robinhood.com stating your intent to close the account. The written email step is required per Robinhood's process. A representative will follow up within 5 business days.
known_friction: Robinhood requires all positions to be liquidated or transferred before account closure — you cannot simply close and have them handle it. For investors with diversified portfolios, this can take significant time and may trigger taxable events. | Retention tactics: None — account closure is straightforward once balances are zero.

free_alternatives.name: Fidelity (free trading)
free_alternatives.note: Commission-free trading with better research tools

notes: Robinhood account closure is permanent. For ACATS transfer (moving investments to another broker without selling), initiate the transfer at the receiving broker — this avoids triggering taxable events. Robinhood Gold ($5/month) cancels separately through the app before account closure.

name: TurboTax
cancel_steps: TurboTax is primarily pay-per-filing, not a recurring subscription. Most users pay once per tax year when they file. If you have TurboTax Advantage (auto-delivery of desktop software) or TurboTax Live, that is a recurring subscription.
To cancel TurboTax Advantage: sign in at myturbotax.intuit.com > select 'My Account' > 'Manage TurboTax Advantage' > Cancel.

To remove TurboTax Live/Expert Assist from your current return (before paying): go to your return > Review Your Order > Downgrade. If you've already used the Expert feature, you cannot remove it from that return.
known_friction: TurboTax aggressively upsells to higher tiers and Expert Assist during the filing process. The downgrade path is intentionally less visible than the upgrade path. | Retention tactics: May offer a discount on TurboTax Advantage renewal.

free_alternatives.name: IRS Free File
free_alternatives.note: Free federal filing for income under $79,000

notes: TurboTax's main dark pattern is upselling during the filing flow, not the subscription model. Most people pay per filing — if you're only filing once a year, there's no recurring subscription to cancel. TurboTax Advantage is the auto-renewing subscription for desktop software delivery.

name: YNAB (You Need A Budget)
cancel_steps: Go to app.youneedabudget.com and sign in.
Click your account name > 'My Account' > 'Cancel your subscription'. If subscribed via iTunes, cancel through Apple Settings > Subscriptions instead.
Confirm cancellation. Access continues through end of billing period. Your budget data is retained for 30 days after cancellation, then deleted.
